patterns_recognized: ARI@LAD: F3 lineup signal has systematically over-rated LAD's lineup in 3 straight tracked losses to ARI pitching (6/10, 7/10 9-3, 7/11 9-2) — treat as a categorical SKIP / fade-toward-ARI if this fixture scores later today with market edge more negative than -10pp, regardless of F3 magnitude.

Large Stage 1/2 disagreement (f5_full_delta) no longer reliably points toward Stage 1's preferred team — the last two slates (7/10, 7/11) both saw Stage 2/full-game direction win even when SP-xERA also opposed the pick (SEA@TB, LAA@MIN). Do not use delta alone to justify an OVERRIDE; SKIP suppression via existing caps remains the correct response.

HARD CAP 7 fired again on a marginal +0.0189 bullpen-xwOBA gap for KC@BAL — nearly identical to the +0.018 gap that broke its 0-for-7 backtest on this exact fixture two days ago (7/10, BAL won 5-3). Applying a cautious partial rescue to GOLD pending a larger sample before trusting the cap's sub-0.04 sensitivity.

HARD CAP 10 four-dimension rescue rule (F-signal + SP-xERA agree + PQI confirm + Stage agree, pre-cap score>=5) is now validated on three occasions, twice on this exact CLE@MIA fixture (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA). Reapplied today at pre-cap score=10 with CLE having also won both recent head-to-head meetings outright.

D-grade picks with both SP-xERA and PQI opposing the pick keep splitting roughly 50/50 directionally across the archive (PHI@DET, LAA@MIN, OAK@CHW, HOU@TEX, TOR@SD tonight) — continuing to SKIP without fading to the opposing side, per repeated postgame guidance that D-grade opposition should suppress the stake, not flip the pick.

MIL @ PIT.reasoning: PLATINUM was explicitly WITHHELD by the pipeline (SP edge 0.69 xERA against MIL — Skenes on the mound; PQI against Δ+7.2). Stage 1/2 disagree by 0.216 (F5 favors PIT ~67%, full game favors MIL only 54.6%). PIT has also beaten MIL in both of the last two head-to-head games (7/11 G1/G2). No live news offsets the ace-SP and recency headwinds. SKIP confirmed.

KC @ BAL.reasoning: HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0189, nearly identical to the +0.018 gap that broke CAP 7's 0-for-7 backtest in this exact KC@BAL fixture on 7/10 (BAL won 5-3). Underlying stack is strong: pre-cap score=5, SP edge 0.90 xERA agrees, PQI confirms Δ+15.8, Stage agree. Cautious partial rescue to GOLD given only one prior data point.

NYY @ WSH.reasoning: NYY has swept the last two head-to-head meetings vs WSH (5-3 on 7/10, 4-2 on 7/11). Today's stack is thin (SP edge unconfirmed, F-signal fires) with HARD CAP 4 correctly flagging Stage1/2 disagreement (Δ=0.28) plus confidence_downgrade — keep tier modest rather than full PLATINUM.

CHC @ CIN.reasoning: Clean but low-conviction pick: PQI and Stage agree, but edge is flat/negative (-1.34pp) and HARD CAP 10 correctly suppresses the bottom-bucket pick_prob (0.549). CHC beat CIN 5-3 on 7/11 (different SP pairing today), mildly supportive. No reason to deviate from SKIP.

BOS @ NYM.reasoning: Part of tonight's repeated pattern: Stage 1 rates the home team (NYM) at 65.5% F5, but the full-game model flips to the away pick (BOS). F-signal and PQI both support BOS, but recent slates show Stage 2 direction has been reliable on its own without needing to fade it — HARD CAP 10 correctly keeps this at SKIP given the thin four-dimension stack.

SEA @ TB.reasoning: Cleanest stack on the slate: SP edge 1.12 xERA agrees, PQI confirms strongly (Δ+17.3), Stage agree, no hard caps fired. The starred F1_xera_gap (thin Ian Seymour sample) is properly corroborated by the strong PQI confirmation per the HARD CAP 5 secondary-confirmation rule, so the star is not disqualifying here.

PHI @ DET.reasoning: SP xERA and PQI both oppose DET. The last two meetings split evenly (DET won 10-2 on 7/10, PHI won 4-2 on 7/11) — a genuine coin-flip matchup. Per the archive's D-grade dual-opposition rule, this should suppress the stake, not flip the pick toward PHI. SKIP confirmed.

CLE @ MIA.reasoning: Textbook HARD CAP 10 rescue: pre-cap score=10 with all four dimensions affirming (F-signal, SP edge agrees, PQI confirms Δ-4.2, Stage agree) — the exact profile validated twice already (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 this same CLE@MIA fixture, both wins). CLE also won both of the last two head-to-head meetings outright (3-2 on 7/10, 4-1 on 7/11). Rescue to full PLATINUM.

LAA @ MIN.reasoning: D-grade with SP edge against, team_quality modifier against, and Stage 1/2 disagreement (Δ=0.14). On 7/11 the model picked the other side (MIN) and MIN won 5-3 comfortably. Today's flip to LAA on an already-weak stack reinforces staying at SKIP with no fade toward MIN.

OAK @ CHW.reasoning: SP xERA (2.00 against) and PQI (-31.0) both strongly oppose CHW, but CHW has won both of the last two tracked meetings vs OAK (14-1 on 7/10, 1-0 on 7/11), suggesting a real team-quality gap the signals aren't capturing. Directionally supportive but the D-grade stack keeps this a firm SKIP rather than an elevation.

ATL @ STL.reasoning: Danny Young (STL) has only 46 Statcast pitches season-to-date, below the 100-pitch xERA-scoring gate. No directional pick is possible yet — the pipeline is correct to withhold. Revisit on the next intraday refresh once SP data clears.

HOU @ TEX.reasoning: PLATINUM was explicitly WITHHELD by the pipeline itself (SP edge 0.81 xERA against pick, PQI against Δ+8.1, Stage 1/2 disagree by 0.21). HOU won the last meeting 9-3, mildly supportive of today's HOU pick, but the internally conflicting stack keeps this at SKIP.

COL @ SF.reasoning: Pre-cap score=8, one of the strongest stacks on the slate: starred F1 corroborated by F3 and F5_bullpen, SP edge agrees, PQI confirms Δ+15.9. Only knock is Stage 1/2 disagreement (Δ=0.12) plus confidence_downgrade triggering the well-validated HARD CAP 4. SF also won 4-2 on 7/11 on a similarly strong stack blocked only by a marginal negative edge. Modest rescue to GOLD, not full PLATINUM, out of respect for CAP 4.

ARI @ LAD.reasoning: Mitch Bratt (LAD) has only 54 Statcast pitches season-to-date, below the 100-pitch gate — no pick yet. Flagging for later refreshes: ARI has beaten LAD in 3 straight tracked games (6/10, 7/10 9-3, 7/11 9-2), with LAD's F3 lineup signal systematically over-rated each time. If this scores later with LAD favored and market edge more negative than -10pp, treat as a categorical SKIP or fade toward ARI regardless of signal strength.

TOR @ SD.reasoning: SP xERA (1.74 against) and PQI (-27.6) both oppose SD, but SD won the last meeting 8-7 on a nearly identical D-grade opposing profile. Directionally supportive, but per the D-grade dual-opposition rule the process response remains SKIP, not an elevation.
